name: [BUG-OT-003] TC-OT-011 — DELETE /token/cache: No auth returns 204 (unauthenticated eviction allowed)
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-OT-003]**

**What this test does:**
Calls DELETE /api/v1/token/cache without any Authorization header and verifies the server rejects the request with 401.

**Expected:** HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**Actual:** HTTP 204 No Content — the cache is evicted without any authentication!

**Impact (DoS Risk):** Any unauthenticated attacker can repeatedly call this endpoint to evict the cached KCB token. Each eviction forces the service to re-authenticate with the KCB authorization server, which:
1. Adds latency to every downstream token request
2. Can trigger KCB rate-limiting if called rapidly
3. May cause cascading failures if the KCB auth server is slow or unavailable

[KNOWN BUG — BUG-OT-003]

What this test does:
Calls DELETE /api/v1/token/cache without any Authorization header and verifies the server rejects the request with 401.

Expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Actual: HTTP 204 No Content — the cache is evicted without any authentication!

Impact (DoS Risk): Any unauthenticated attacker can repeatedly call this endpoint to evict the cached KCB token. Each eviction forces the service to re-authenticate with the KCB authorization server, which:

  1. Adds latency to every downstream token request
  2. Can trigger KCB rate-limiting if called rapidly
  3. May cause cascading failures if the KCB auth server is slow or unavailable
